commit.message:
feat: support running personal_mtproxy on back node in split-mode setup

pm_registry now broadcasts mtp_policy_table updates to all front nodes
instead of calling the local process directly. This makes DETS the single
source of truth in multi-front deployments: any front that (re)connects
gets the full subdomain list replayed automatically.

Changes:
- pm_registry: replace direct mtp_policy_table calls with broadcast_policy/3
  which erpc-calls every cached front node
- pm_registry: monitor_nodes(true) in init; nodeup/nodedown handlers
  maintain a front_nodes cache (checked via whereis(mtp_policy_table))
- pm_registry: refresh_fronts/0 — re-scans nodes() and replays into any
  newly discovered fronts; useful after split-brain recovery
- personal_mtproxy_app: cowboy_listen_addr/0 gives a clear error when
  domain_fronting is absent and no explicit listen addr is configured
  (required on back nodes which don't have domain_fronting)
- README: split-mode setup section with front and back config snippets

Back node requires ports in sys.config (personal_mtproxy reads port +
secret for link generation; mtproto_proxy ignores it on back nodes).
Front nodes run vanilla mtproto_proxy; domain_fronting must point to
the back node's Cowboy address, not localhost.

Compatible with node_role = both (degenerates to a local erpc call,
no behaviour change for single-node deployments).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: upgrade Go toolchain to 1.26.2 to resolve stdlib vulnerabilities (#276)

## Summary
- Bumps `go.mod` from `go 1.26.1` to `go 1.26.2` to resolve 5 Go
standard library vulnerabilities
- `govulncheck` now reports 0 vulnerabilities (down from 5)
- Build and `go vet` pass cleanly with go1.26.2

## Vulnerabilities Fixed

| ID | Package | Description |
|----|---------|-------------|
| GO-2026-4947 | `crypto/x509` | Unexpected work during chain building |
| GO-2026-4946 | `crypto/x509` | Inefficient policy validation |
| GO-2026-4870 | `crypto/tls` | Unauthenticated TLS 1.3 KeyUpdate causes
DoS |
| GO-2026-4866 | `crypto/x509` | Case-sensitive excludedSubtrees name
constraints auth bypass |
| GO-2026-4865 | `html/template` | JsBraceDepth context tracking bugs
(XSS) |

## Changes
- `go-api/go.mod`: `go 1.26.1` → `go 1.26.2` (1-line change)

## Validation
- `govulncheck ./...`: 0 vulnerabilities
- `go build ./...`: pass
- `go vet ./...`: pass

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)


## Follow-up commit
- Switched CI Go setup from \`go-version: '1.26'\` to \`go-version-file:
go-api/go.mod\` per Copilot review feedback, so the exact patched
toolchain is always used.

---------

Add AuthRepository scaffold and refactor pgsql.DB to OpenDB function (#12)

* Add AuthRepository scaffold and refactor pgsql.DB to OpenDB function

Replace the pgsql.DB wrapper struct with a single OpenDB function that
returns *sql.DB directly. The wrapper only existed for lifecycle
management (pool config + open/close), but callers needed the underlying
*sql.DB anyway — for repository construction and transactions. This is
the conventional Go pattern (used by sqlx, GORM, and recommended by
Alex Edwards' Let's Go Further).

Also adds the AuthRepository struct and constructor that accepts *sql.DB.
Query methods are implemented in follow-up PRs (6b–6e); the compile-time
interface check is added in 6e once all methods exist.

Add dot product dilution insight to high-dimension limitations

Explains that while the full PE vector is unique, the Q/K dot product
is a sum of per-dimension contributions — near-zero positional
differences in high-i dimensions dilute the signal from useful
low-i dimensions.

feat: smooth image gallery swipe and page transition animations (#208)

- Fix root cause of flash/pop in gallery: remove CSS opacity classes that
  conflict with framer-motion inline-style opacity (inline wins, making
  the class-based system a no-op; image content popped in after slide
  animation). Framer-motion now exclusively owns opacity.
- Animate skeleton fade-out via AnimatePresence + Motion.div exit so the
  loading state dissolves smoothly rather than vanishing instantly.
- Add will-change: transform, opacity to gallery img, lightbox img, and
  lightbox inner container for GPU layer promotion.
- Tune gallery spring: stiffness 380→340, damping 38→44, mass 0.8→0.75
  (removes overshoot, keeps slide crisp). Exit travel reduced to 80% so
  the outgoing image clears faster. Opacity ease → material [0.4,0,0.2,1].
- Unify PageTransition enter easing with ProductModal ([0.22,1,0.36,1]),
  duration 0.25→0.28s, exit ease → [0.36,0,0.66,0]. Drift 10→12 / 6→8px.
- ProductModal backdrop: add ease [0.4,0,0.2,1]. Panel: add will-change.

Expand cards with multiple TCGPlayer variants into separate checklist entries (#21)

* Expand cards with multiple TCGPlayer variants into separate entries

Previously, one TCG API card mapped to one checklist entry. But
many cards have multiple TCGPlayer price types (normal + reverseHolofoil,
holofoil + reverseHolofoil, 1stEditionHolofoil + unlimitedHolofoil).
For a master set, each variant needs its own checklist entry.

Now mapTCGCardToCards() expands a single API card into multiple
PokemonCard entries — one per price type variant. Each gets a unique
ID (e.g., ex7-67-reverseHolofoil), correct variant type, variant
label, and individual pricing.

This fixes missing entries for:
- Reverse holo / stamped variants (e.g., EX Team Rocket Returns Mareep)
- 1st Edition vs Unlimited printings (e.g., Neo Destiny Dark Ampharos)
- Normal vs Holofoil versions

Cameo cards are not expanded (one entry per cameo appearance).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: preserve semantic variant (promo/collab/etc) for expanded cards

Review feedback: mapVariant() was bypassed for cards with multiple
price types, losing promo/collab/tournament/secret-rare/etc variant
classification. Now uses mapVariant() as base variant and only falls
back to physical variant (holo/reverse-holo) when the card has no
more specific classification.
